subject: [RFC PATCH 00/10] mm/swap: ghost swapfile with backend switching via Redirect entries

reviews.author: Chris Li
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Chris Li pointed out that the patch still uses an xarray for lookup swap entry to cluster, which he wants to eliminate in favor of a solution where no xarray is used in zswap/VS lookup of swap entries.

Reviewer Chris Li noted that the patch series needs two things from the swap core function: the ability to allocate more swap entries beyond the current si->max, which can be achieved by Baoquan's kvmalloc grow patches, and the ability to store private data structures per swap slot, such as a zswap entry pointer or redirection pointer. He suggested modifying the swap core to provide extra context per swap entry for each swap device, using the same kvmalloc grow trick to grow the vs_table array.

Reviewer Chris Li expressed caution and skepticism towards the patch series, citing past experience with similar claims of metadata overhead savings, and requested further review before making any conclusions.

Reviewer Chris Li noted that the proposed swap entry would require an extra xarray lookup to access cluster info, which he prefers to avoid in favor of a direct array lookup, as made possible by Baoquan's kvmalloc grow

Reviewer Chris Li noted that the patch still includes an xarray lookup in the code path, which he wants to eliminate, and requested clarification on his previous concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
But you still have one xarray for lookup swap entry to cluster right?

I want to get rid of xarray in both zswap and VS. The ultimate
solution is to not have xarray in the zswap/VS lookup of swap entries
at all. I believe achieving that would be a better win. I can already
see that happening by building on top of Baoquan's xswap kvmalloc
growth series.

---

I mean, here is my observation, please correct me if I am wrong,  VS
patche series needs two things from the swap core function:
1) able to allocate more swap entry beyond the current si->max.
Baoqun's kvmalloc grow patches should be able to answer that.
2) able to store private data structures per swap slot. e.g. a zswap
entry pointer, or a redirection pointer or both. We can modify the
swap core to provide this extra context per swap entry for each swap
device. In Baoquan's new purposal that is the vs_table array. We can
use the same kvmalloc grow trick to grow this pointer array as well.

That way, it does not need to have the VS xarray lookup in front of
cluster_info. You just keep growing the cluster info array and
vs_table array as needed.

---

Pending my deeper look into your patch series. I have been bitten by
your patch series' claim about metadata overhead before so I am a bit
more cautious about concluding anything regarding your patch's meta
data usage now :-)

---

It is not the case in my mind because the swap entry will require an
extra xarray lookup to get to the cluster info. No?
I do  not want that extra xarray lookup. I prefer an array lookup if
possible. With Baoquan's kvmalloc grow, that should be possible.

---

It still has the xarray lookup in the code path (looking up the swap
entry index to get swap cluster info), that xarray is what I want to
get rid of.

Chris

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch proposes a new way to implement ghost swapfiles, allowing for backend switching via Redirect entries. The approach aims to minimize changes to the existing data structures and operations, keeping them as close to the current implementation as possible. This is in contrast to an alternative proposal that would require significant modifications to the swap cluster and table data structures.
